ranked_trials.title: A Study of Amivantamab in Combination With Lazertinib, or Amivantamab in Combination With Platinum-Based Chemotherapy, for Common Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR)-Mutated Locally Advanced or Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)
ranked_trials.brief_summary: The primary purpose of the study is to assess how well amivantamab in combination with lazertinib or in combination with chemotherapy works (antitumor activity) in participants with epidermal growth factor receptor mutated (EGFRm) non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC; that is one of the major types of lung cancer).

ranked_trials.patient_summary:
WHAT THIS TRIAL IS TESTING:
This study looks at two new drug combinations to see how well they shrink or control non-small cell lung cancer in people with specific genetic markers (EGFR mutations).

WHY THIS MIGHT BE RIGHT FOR YOU:
* Your specific lung cancer mutation (EGFR exon 19 deletion) is a direct match for what these drugs are designed to target.
* You have already received some standard treatments, and this trial offers a different approach to managing your cancer.
* The study is open to people with your stage of lung cancer who are looking for new options.

WHAT WOULD BE INVOLVED:
You would receive either a combination of two targeted drugs (amivantamab and lazertinib) or one targeted drug plus chemotherapy. You will need to visit the study site regularly for treatments, check-ups, and scans to see how the medicine is working.

IMPORTANT TO CONFIRM WITH YOUR DOCTOR:
Please ask your doctor if you meet the specific requirements for "measurable lesions" on your scans. Also, confirm that any side effects from your past chemotherapy have healed enough for you to start a new treatment. Finally, ensure you have not had major surgery in the last four weeks.
ranked_trials.physician_brief:
**TRIAL:** NCT06667076; A Study of Amivantamab in Combination With Lazertinib or Chemotherapy for EGFR-Mutated NSCLC; Phase 2; Sponsor: Janssen.

**CLINICAL RATIONALE:** Patient is a 58F with Stage IIIB EGFR Exon 19del+ NSCLC. Following progression on platinum-doublet therapy, this trial offers a targeted approach using the bispecific antibody amivantamab combined with the third-generation TKI lazertinib or chemotherapy, addressing the need for next-line EGFR-directed therapy.

**KEY ELIGIBILITY:** 
*   **Confirmed:** Histology, EGFR Ex19del status, ECOG 1.
*   **Uncertain:** Requires verification of RECIST v1.1 measurable disease, resolution of prior treatment toxicities to Grade ≤1, and absence of uncontrolled tumor-related pain.

**LOGISTICS:** Site location TBD; requires frequent monitoring for infusion-related reactions and potential ILD.

**RECOMMENDATION:** **Strong Match.** The patient’s molecular profile and prior treatment history align well with the trial’s focus on overcoming resistance in EGFR-mutated NSCLC.
ranked_trials.outreach_email:
Subject: Inquiry regarding clinical trial NCT06667076

Dear Clinical Trial Coordinator,

I am writing to express my interest in participating in the Phase 2 study of Amivantamab (NCT06667076). 

I am a patient currently residing in Boston, MA, diagnosed with Stage IIIB Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer. My tumor is EGFR exon 19 deletion positive with a PD-L1 expression of 40%. My prior treatment includes Carboplatin and Paclitaxel, and I currently maintain an ECOG performance status of 1.

Given my specific mutation profile and treatment history, I believe I may be a strong candidate for this trial. Could you please confirm if this study is currently enrolling new participants? If so, I would appreciate information regarding the next steps for pre-screening or the process for having my records reviewed.

Thank you for your time and for your work in advancing these therapies.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]

ranked_trials.title: Solid Tumor Analysis for HLA Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH) and Apheresis for CAR T- Cell Manufacturing
ranked_trials.brief_summary:
Objective:

To collect information on how often a solid tumor cancer might lose the Human Leukocyte Antigen (HLA) by next generation sequencing and perform apheresis to collect and store an eligible participant's own T cells for future use to make CAR T-Cell therapy for their disease treatment.

Design:

This is a non-interventional, observational study to evaluate participants with solid tumors with a high risk of relapse for incurable disease. No interventional therapy will be administered on this study. Some of the information regarding the participant's tumor analysis may be beneficial to management of their disease. Participants that meet all criteria may be enrolled and leukapheresed (blood cells collected). The participant's cells will be processed and stored for potential manufacture of CAR T-cell therapy upon relapse of their cancer.

ranked_trials.patient_summary:
WHAT THIS TRIAL IS TESTING:
This study is collecting information about your tumor’s genetics. It also involves collecting your own immune cells (T-cells) to store them for potential use in a future cancer treatment called CAR T-cell therapy.

WHY THIS MIGHT BE RIGHT FOR YOU:
* It is a way to prepare your body’s own cells for a new type of treatment that may be available later.
* You have a diagnosis that matches the study’s focus on solid tumors.
* The study is observational, meaning it does not involve taking a new experimental drug right now.

WHAT WOULD BE INVOLVED:
You will have a blood draw and a procedure called apheresis. During apheresis, blood is taken from one arm, passed through a machine to collect your immune cells, and returned to your other arm. This is a one-time collection.

IMPORTANT TO CONFIRM WITH YOUR DOCTOR:
* You must have a specific genetic marker called "HLA-A*02" to qualify.
* Your tumor tissue must be tested to see if it has lost a specific protein.
* You must not have active cancer spread to your brain.
* Ask your doctor if your recent treatments fit the study’s timing requirements.
ranked_trials.physician_brief:
**TRIAL:** NCT04981119; Solid Tumor Analysis for HLA Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH) and Apheresis for CAR T-Cell Manufacturing; Phase: N/A; Sponsor: A2 Biotherapeutics, Inc.

**CLINICAL RATIONALE:** Patient is a 58F with Stage IIIB EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Exon 19 del) progressing post-platinum doublet. This trial evaluates HLA-A*02 LOH status to determine eligibility for T-cell therapy targeting tumor-specific HLA loss, offering a potential novel therapeutic avenue for patients with limited remaining standard-of-care options.

**KEY ELIGIBILITY:** 
*   **Confirmed:** ECOG 1, no prior allogeneic/organ transplant, no recent malignancy.
*   **Uncertain:** Requires HLA-A*02 heterozygosity and tumor-specific HLA-A*02 LOH confirmation via NGS. CNS metastasis status and washout period from prior systemic therapy require verification.

**LOGISTICS:** Site location and visit schedule TBD pending sponsor coordination.

**RECOMMENDATION:** **Moderate Match.** The patient meets baseline performance and diagnostic criteria, but candidacy is strictly dependent on specific HLA-A*02 molecular profiling.

ranked_trials.title: Prophylactic Anti-Seizure Medication vs No Anti-Seizure Medication for Patients With Primary Motor Cortex Brain Metastases
ranked_trials.brief_summary: This is a randomized trial for patients with brain metastases in the primary motor cortex who have not had seizures to receive either the prophylactic anti-seizure medication levetiracetam (also known by its trade name Keppra) or proceed with standard of care management, which does not currently include prophylactic levetiracetam. Patients who enroll to this trial will be randomized to receive prophylactic levetiracetam or not receive prophylactic levetiracetam.

ranked_trials.patient_summary:
WHAT THIS TRIAL IS TESTING:
This study looks at whether taking a seizure-prevention medicine (Keppra) helps patients who have lung cancer that has spread to a specific part of the brain that controls movement. It compares patients taking this medicine to those who do not take it.

WHY THIS MIGHT BE RIGHT FOR YOU:
* You have a diagnosis that may qualify for this study if you have a brain lesion in the motor cortex.
* You have not had seizures before, which is a key requirement for this trial.
* It offers a chance to help researchers learn if preventative medicine can improve quality of life for patients with your condition.

WHAT WOULD BE INVOLVED:
You will be randomly assigned to either take Keppra or receive standard care without the medicine. The study involves regular clinic visits to monitor your health and brain activity.

IMPORTANT TO CONFIRM WITH YOUR DOCTOR:
* Do you have a brain lesion in the motor cortex that is at least 0.5 cm?
* Are you allergic to Keppra? (If yes, you cannot join).
* Do you have any trouble getting an MRI scan? (If yes, you cannot join).
* Confirm your overall physical health status with your oncology team.
ranked_trials.physician_brief:
**TRIAL:** NCT07130786, Prophylactic Anti-Seizure Medication vs. No Anti-Seizure Medication for Patients With Primary Motor Cortex Brain Metastases, Phase 2, Sponsor: Ayal A. Aizer, MD.

**CLINICAL RATIONALE:** While the patient has EGFR-mutated Stage IIIB NSCLC, this trial is specific to patients with intracranial lesions in the primary motor cortex. The patient’s current staging does not explicitly confirm brain metastases, which is a mandatory inclusion criterion.

**KEY ELIGIBILITY:** 
*   **Confirmed:** Age ≥18, ECOG 1 (consistent with KPS ≥60).
*   **Uncertain:** Presence of biopsy-proven primary motor cortex intracranial lesion (≥0.5 cm). 

**LOGISTICS:** Nearest site unknown; requires verification of local availability.

**RECOMMENDATION: Weak Match.** The trial is indicated only for patients with specific primary motor cortex metastases; unless current imaging confirms this finding, the patient is ineligible.

ranked_trials.title: DNA Analysis of Blood and Tissue from Patients with Lung Cancer
ranked_trials.brief_summary:
RATIONALE: Studying samples of blood and tissue from patients with cancer in the laboratory may help doctors learn more about changes that occur in DNA and identify biomarkers related to cancer. It may also help the study of cancer in the future.

PURPOSE: This clinical trial is analyzing the DNA in blood and tissue samples from patients with lung cancer.
